title: Formal curved OR Juhl assembly
summary: Composes a stored formal Juhl statement with a stored formal Juhl–OR bridge and instantiates the construction at orders one and two using only identity bridges.

mathematicalContent.heading: Assembly principle
mathematicalContent.body: If formalP N equals a Juhl RHS and that RHS equals an OR RHS under normalized syntax equality, transitivity gives formalP N equal to the OR side.
mathematicalContent.formula: P_{2N}^{\mathrm{formal}}\equiv R_J,\quad R_J\equiv R_{\mathrm{OR}}\quad\Longrightarrow\quad P_{2N}^{\mathrm{formal}}\equiv R_{\mathrm{OR}}.

mathematicalContent.heading: Low-order instances
mathematicalContent.body: At orders one and two the OR bridge is the identity bridge, so the OR side is simply the same raw expression already stored in the formal Juhl statement.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a formal Juhl statement, a formal OR bridge, and an equality identifying the bridge's Juhl side with the statement's RHS.

correspondence.leanType: def orSide {N : ℕ} (F : CurvedORJuhlTypeFormula N) : FormalExpr
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Extracts the formal expression on the OR side of the stored bridge.

correspondence.leanType: theorem formula {N : ℕ} (F : CurvedORJuhlTypeFormula N) : FormalEq (formalP N) F.orSide
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves the formal curved OR identity by transitivity of the two stored normalized syntax equalities.
Builds the order-one assembly using the order-one raw statement and the identity bridge.
States the formal order-one identity obtained from that assembly.
Builds the raw order-two assembly using the order-two raw statement and the identity bridge.
States the formal raw order-two identity obtained from that assembly.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: The theorem is formal transitive assembly from equalities stored in the input structures. The low-order bridges are identities, not comparisons with concrete OR operators. No general curved Juhl formula, actual coefficient correspondence, geometric evaluation, or bottom-up operator theorem is proved.
